title: ranunculus nemorosus proles mixtus
intro: Ranunculus nemorosus proles mixtus is a subspecies of the Ranunculus nemorosus plant, a perennial species that is a hybrid of the lesser celandine (Ranunculus ficaria) and the wood buttercup (Ranunculus nemorosus). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus nemorosus proles mixtus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate skin irritations, and treat wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
